source_note: Source-level modality lock for the uui authentication / conflict / generic-error interaction surface (WIN-SYS-011). It does not add or restyle any dialog: the ten uui .ui roots already render through the shared vcl parts covered by definition.xml and the dialog-anatomy contract. It pins, fail-closed, that every uui interaction dialog stays modal (native-exclusion) by re-running the shared NotificationRouter classifier on the live .ui and three-way cross-checking the shared dialog-notification-policy.csv row, proves the four credential dialogs hit the credential precedence branch via a visibility=False password GtkEntry, pins the modal conflict/error ->run() call sites, and pins the informational-error routing seam with routing declared seam-only-not-wired. It claims no native build, dialog pixels, keyboard/interaction, or any runtime evidence; runtime_verified stays false and no uui notification producer is wired.

conflict_sites.note: Name-clash error box (NameClashDialog::SameName) is presented modally via weld::MessageDialog ->run().
handleLoadReadOnlyRequest resolves the read-only/edit conflict with a modal ->run()==RET_YES decision.
Lock/reload conflict query boxes resolve modally via ->run().

error_seam.note: isInformationalErrorMessageRequest is the routing seam: a request is informational-only iff it has exactly one continuation that is Approve or Abort. The generic error handler still presents every error modally via executeErrorDialog->run(); no outcome is routed to the bottom-right stack today.

routing_carveout.note: No uui NotificationRouter producer exists. The informational-error seam (isInformationalErrorMessageRequest) is present but unwired; a future producer would route only informational-only outcomes, keeping every interactive/credential/conflict prompt modal. Promoting this status to 'wired' without a real producer must fail the contract.

shared_pins.note: Macro-security warning overlapping WIN-SYS-006/007; referenced here read-only for its modality only (native-exclusion). Its macro-security assertion is owned by another row and is NOT claimed here.
SSL trust-decision prompt overlapping WIN-SYS-007; pinned here only as a modal decision prompt (native-exclusion), not as an owned trust-decision redesign.
